question: In Nature journal's Scientific Reports conference proceedings from 2012, in the article that did not mention plasmons or plasmonics, what nano-compound is studied? Don't use the prefix nano in your answer if there is one.

research_queries: Nature Scientific Reports 2012 full article list
working_plan: Search for Nature's Scientific Reports conference proceedings from 2012
Locate and access the full proceedings or comprehensive article listings
Identify all articles that study nano-compounds or nanomaterials
Filter out any articles that mention 'plasmons' or 'plasmonics' in title/abstract/content
Extract the specific nano-compound name from the remaining qualifying article(s)
Remove 'nano' prefix from the answer if present

solver.reasoning_summary: Found 4 conference proceedings in Scientific Reports 2012. Two mentioned 'plasmonic' in titles, one remaining article mentioned 'surface plasmon' in content, leaving only the diamond photonic crystal article which studies nano-diamond without mentioning plasmons/plasmonics.

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Searched "nature scientific reports" on Google.
2. Opened https://www.nature.com/srep/.
3. Selected Explore Content > Research Articles.
4. Filtered for Conference Proceedings from 2012.
5. Opened each article link.
6. Checked for "plasmon" or "plasmonic".
7. Noted the nano-compound in the article that did not include either.
